title: dysphania rhadinostachya subsp. rhadinostachya
intro: Dysphania rhadinostachya subsp. rhadinostachya, also known as New Zealand spinach or Tetragonia, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Dysphania rhadinostachya subsp. rhadinostachya can be prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
